testcase.name: test_14_01_budget_correctness_every_forward_step

failure.message: section14_runtime.Section14DiscoveryError: No compatible synthetic input was found for the discovered model. Attempts: shape=(2, 12), dtype=torch.int64: ValueError: inputs must have shape [batch, sequence, input_dim] shape=(2, 12, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16 shape=(2, 12, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16 shape=(12, 2), dtype=torch.int64: ValueError: inputs must have shape [batch, sequence, input_dim] shape=(12, 2, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16

testcase.failure:
def test_14_01_budget_correctness_every_forward_step() -> None: budget = 4 model = build_model(seed=2026, budget=budget) model.eval() encourage_writes(model) > x = compatible_input(model, seq_len=12) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_section14_required.py:72: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
model = BudgetMemR( (recurrent_cell): GRUCell(8, 16) (key_projection): Linear(in_features=16, out_features=8, bias=False) ...ures=64, bias=True) (1): SiLU() (2): Linear(in_features=64, out_features=1, bias=True) (3): Sigmoid() ) ) seq_len = 12
def compatible_input(model: nn.Module, seq_len: int = 12) -> Tensor: failures: list[str] = [] for x in _candidate_inputs(model, seq_len=seq_len): try: reset_memory(model, require=False) with torch.no_grad(): invoke(model, x, reset=True) return x except Exception as exc: failures.append(f"shape={tuple(x.shape)}, dtype={x.dtype}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}") > raise Section14DiscoveryError( "No compatible synthetic input was found for the discovered model. Attempts:\n" + "\n".join(failures) ) E section14_runtime.Section14DiscoveryError: No compatible synthetic input was found for the discovered model. Attempts: E shape=(2, 12), dtype=torch.int64: ValueError: inputs must have shape [batch, sequence, input_dim] E shape=(2, 12, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16 E shape=(2, 12, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16 E shape=(12, 2), dtype=torch.int64: ValueError: inputs must have shape [batch, sequence, input_dim] E shape=(12, 2, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16
tests/section14_runtime.py:438: Section14DiscoveryError

failure.message: section14_runtime.Section14DiscoveryError: No compatible synthetic input was found for the discovered model. Attempts: shape=(2, 12), dtype=torch.int64: ValueError: inputs must have shape [batch, sequence, input_dim] shape=(2, 12, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16 shape=(2, 12, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16 shape=(12, 2), dtype=torch.int64: ValueError: inputs must have shape [batch, sequence, input_dim] shape=(12, 2, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16

testcase.failure:
def test_14_02_causality_future_tokens_do_not_change_earlier_steps() -> None: seed = 2026 model_a = build_model(seed=seed, budget=4) model_b = build_model(seed=seed, budget=4) assert state_dict_equal(model_a, model_b), "Models are not identically initialized."
model_a.eval() model_b.eval() > x = compatible_input(model_a, seq_len=12) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

def compatible_input(model: nn.Module, seq_len: int = 12) -> Tensor: failures: list[str] = [] for x in _candidate_inputs(model, seq_len=seq_len): try: reset_memory(model, require=False) with torch.no_grad(): invoke(model, x, reset=True) return x except Exception as exc: failures.append(f"shape={tuple(x.shape)}, dtype={x.dtype}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}") > raise Section14DiscoveryError( "No compatible synthetic input was found for the discovered model. Attempts:\n" + "\n".join(failures) ) E section14_runtime.Section14DiscoveryError: No compatible synthetic input was found for the discovered model. Attempts: E shape=(2, 12), dtype=torch.int64: ValueError: inputs must have shape [batch, sequence, input_dim] E shape=(2, 12, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16 E shape=(2, 12, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16 E shape=(12, 2), dtype=torch.int64: ValueError: inputs must have shape [batch, sequence, input_dim] E shape=(12, 2, 16), dtype=torch.float32: ValueError: Expected input_dim=8, received 16
tests/section14_runtime.py:438: Section14DiscoveryError

testcase.name: test_14_03_deterministic_dataset_generation
testcase.failure:
def test_14_03_deterministic_dataset_generation() -> None: try: first = build_synthetic_dataset(seed=2026, split="train") second = build_synthetic_dataset(seed=2026, split="train") except Section14DiscoveryError: assert existing_test_has( ("synthetic", "dataset"), ("determin", "same seed", "reproduc"), ), ( "Synthetic dataset factory was not discovered and no existing deterministic " "synthetic-dataset test was found." ) return
> assert dataset_fingerprint(first) == dataset_fingerprint(second), ( ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ "The same synthetic seed and configuration produced different datasets." )
tests/test_section14_required.py:157: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
dataset = {'input': array([[294, 0], [612, 0], [ 69, 1], [128, 2], [715, 0], [860, ..., 3]]), 'target': array([191]), 'relevant_positions': [14, 15], 'distractor_positions': [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ...], ...} limit = 16
def dataset_fingerprint(dataset: Any, limit: int = 16) -> str: digest = hashlib.sha256() if hasattr(dataset, "__len__") and hasattr(dataset, "__getitem__"): count = min(int(len(dataset)), limit) for index in range(count): > digest.update(_stable_serialize(dataset[index])) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E KeyError: 0
tests/section14_runtime.py:850: KeyError

testcase.failure:

Attempts:\n" + "\n".join(failures) ) E section14_runtime.Section14DiscoveryError: No compatible synthetic input was found for the discovered model. testcase.name: test_strict_budget_is_never_violated

failure.message: TypeError: BudgetMemR.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'allowed_budgets'

testcase.failure:
def test_strict_budget_is_never_violated() -> None: torch.manual_seed(2026) > model = _model().eval() ^^^^^^^^
tests/models/test_budgetmem_r.py:32: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
overrides = {}
def _model(**overrides: object) -> BudgetMemR: config: dict[str, object] = { "input_dim": 6, "hidden_dim": 12, "output_dim": 3, "max_budget": 5, "allowed_budgets": (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), "key_dim": 8, "value_dim": 10, "retrieval_k": 2, "write_threshold": 0.0, } config.update(overrides) > return BudgetMemR(**config) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: BudgetMemR.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'allowed_budgets'
tests/models/test_budgetmem_r.py:27: TypeError

testcase.name: test_memory_never_exceeds_configured_budget_at_any_forward_step

failure.message: TypeError: BudgetMemR.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'allowed_budgets'

testcase.failure:
overrides = {}
def build_budgetmem_model(**overrides: object) -> nn.Module: """Build the exact small BudgetMem-R model used by Section 13 tests.""" module = _load_section13_test_module() factory = getattr(module, "_model", None) if factory is None: raise AttributeError( "tests/models/test_budgetmem_r.py must expose the Section 13 " "_model test factory" )
try: > model = factory(**overrides) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

testcase.name: test_same_seed_produces_identical_model_initialization_and_evaluation

failure.message: TypeError: BudgetMemR.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'allowed_budgets'

testcase.failure:
overrides = {}
def build_budgetmem_model(**overrides: object) -> nn.Module: """Build the exact small BudgetMem-R model used by Section 13 tests.""" module = _load_section13_test_module() factory = getattr(module, "_model", None) if factory is None: raise AttributeError( "tests/models/test_budgetmem_r.py must expose the Section 13 " "_model test factory" )
try: > model = factory(**overrides) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/pretraining/conftest.py:47: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
overrides = {}
def _model(**overrides: object) -> BudgetMemR: config: dict[str, object] = { "input_dim": 6, "hidden_dim": 12, "output_dim": 3, "max_budget": 5, "allowed_budgets": (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), "key_dim": 8, "value_dim": 10, "retrieval_k": 2, "write_threshold": 0.0, } config.update(overrides) > return BudgetMemR(**config) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: BudgetMemR.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'allowed_budgets'
tests/models/test_budgetmem_r.py:27: TypeError
During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
def test_same_seed_produces_identical_model_initialization_and_evaluation() -> None: seed_everything(2026) > first_model = build_budgetmem_model().eval() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/pretraining/test_determinism.py:66: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ tests/pretraining/conftest.py:51: in build_budgetmem_model model = factory() ^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
